message:
fix(h2): zero-increment WINDOW_UPDATE on stream is stream error, not connection

Per RFC 9113 §6.9.1, zero increment on a stream must be treated as a
stream error (RST_STREAM) of type PROTOCOL_ERROR, while zero increment
on stream 0 (connection) must be a connection error. The previous code
returned a connection error for both, killing the entire connection
when only one stream misbehaved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1f3nn","title":"[SPORK][API] Ergonomics Layer: Behaviors, Builders, and Zero-Footgun Defaults","notes":"Closeout validation complete: dependency epics are closed (bd-pr46z, bd-3rpp8, bd-2n7yw, bd-1zok9, bd-11z9a) and API child slices are closed (bd-2td4e module/prelude, bd-2sjzv builder defaults, bd-2x5xc error taxonomy, bd-ydk4e ergonomic system messages). Epic acceptance intent (discoverable/stable ergonomic API glue over core Spork primitives) is now represented by landed child work and exported surface/tests/docs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"FoggyMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:41:25.371901865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:19:39.825635448Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:19:39.825561901Z","close_reason":"Completed via closed child slices covering module/prelude exports, builder ergonomics/defaults, unified error taxonomy, and ergonomic system message surface.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","ergonomics","otp","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-11z9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-1jsq9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-1zok9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-2n7yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-3rpp8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","depends_on_id":"bd-pr46z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":856,"issue_id":"bd-1f3nn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Spork API Ergonomics ("Just Works" Surface)

The hard part is not writing a GenServer loop; it's making the *defaults* correct so people don't have to think:

- call/cast semantics that are cancel-safe by construction
- supervision policies that compile to regions
- naming/monitoring that is deterministic and capability-scoped

This epic is the "glue UX" layer:

- builders that compile down to the core primitives
- behavior traits that minimize boilerplate
- zero-footgun defaults (safe until you ask for unsafe)

## Design Constraints

- No ambient authority: all facilities are capabilities on `Cx` or region scopes.
- No hidden runtimes.
- Determinism first: stable ordering and trace visibility.
- Allocation discipline: avoid hot-path heap activity.

 {"id":"bd-1f8jn","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Choreographic Programming for Saga Protocol Generation","description":"Choreographic Programming (§6.13) allows defining a saga as a single global protocol, then automatically projecting it to per-participant code. This eliminates protocol mismatch bugs — the global protocol is the single source of truth. Currently, saga participants are written independently and tested for compatibility. With choreographic compilation, participants are generated from the global spec, making protocol bugs impossible by construction. Key papers: Montesi 2023 'Introduction to Choreographies'. Implementation: Define a choreography DSL for saga protocols. Compile to per-participant Asupersync tasks. Integrate with session types (Epic 1) for type-safe message passing.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:48.027147442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T07:14:23.653543074Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T07:14:23.653409937Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","choreographic","sagas"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn","depends_on_id":"bd-2wrsc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f8jn.1","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Design choreography DSL for Asupersync sagas","description":"Design a choreography DSL for specifying Asupersync saga protocols as global interaction descriptions. Must express: (1) Participant declarations with typed roles, (2) Message sends: A.send(M) -> B, (3) Choice/branching: if A.decides(pred) { branch1 } else { branch2 }, (4) Recursion for repeating steps, (5) Compensation actions for rollback. Embeddable as Rust proc-macro #[choreography] block for IDE support. Must ensure deadlock-freedom by construction. Deliverable: Grammar spec, example protocols, expressiveness analysis. Reference: graveyard section 6.13, Montesi 2023. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: (Design deliverable -- define tracing contract for compiler and runtime.) Span: choreography::parse with source_file, participant_count. Span: choreography::project with protocol_name, target_participant. DEBUG: DSL token parsed: token_type={type}, value={value}, line={line}. INFO: choreography parsed: name={name}, participants={n}, messages={n}, branches={n}. WARN: choreography design issue: {issue_description} (e.g., knowledge-of-choice violation candidate). ERROR: choreography parse error: line={line}, column={col}, expected={expected}, found={found}. Metrics: choreography_parse_time_us (histogram), choreography_participant_count (histogram), choreography_message_count (histogram), choreography_branch_count (histogram).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:57.250145217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T05:41:12.065206430Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T05:41:02.478350401Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","choreographic","design","dsl"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1f8jn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:12Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1430,"issue_id":"bd-1f8jn.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"[2026-02-14 MagentaElk] Choreography DSL designed and implemented (1956 lines). Module: src/obligation/choreography.rs. AST types (GlobalProtocol, Interaction, LocalType), builder API, knowledge-of-choice validator, projection compiler, 5 example protocols, 27 tests. All 6514 lib tests pass, zero clippy warnings.","created_at":"2026-02-14T05:41:12Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-366fd","title":"[LEAN-COVERAGE][T2.2b] Remove brittle substitution/rewrite idioms in unstable branches","description":"Replace fragile proof idioms (invalid subst forms, direction-sensitive rewrite assumptions) with robust local proof patterns.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Target branches no longer fail due to substitution/rewrite direction errors.
- Updated patterns are documented for reuse.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Target branches no longer fail with `subst-failed`, `rewrite-failed`, or equality-orientation mismatch for the addressed class.
2. Replacements use deterministic local proof patterns (constructor-focused `cases`, explicit `rw` direction, explicit witness terms).
3. Pattern guide is documented in bead notes or linked artifact so future fixes follow the same robust style.
4. Frontier deltas show reduction in addressed proof-shape error codes.","notes":"Rationale: brittle idioms create recurring churn and obscure true semantic gaps. This bead codifies robust replacements.

Pattern migration examples:
- avoid ambiguous/global `simp` where explicit local rewrite is clearer
- avoid context-destroying substitutions when preserving key identifiers aids branch proof completion
- split constructor cases earlier to reduce goal-shape ambiguity","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:17:12.166205465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:44:00.032933514Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-366fd","depends_on_id":"bd-53a0d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-366fd","depends_on_id":"bd-vmom8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-36ava","title":"[AUDIT] Deep correctness audit: runtime/scheduler/priority.rs (priority scheduler)","notes":"VERDICT: SOUND. 0 bugs, 0 findings. 2201 lines audited. Three-lane priority scheduler (Cancel>Timed>Ready) using BinaryHeaps. ScheduledSet hybrid dense+overflow dedup (tag encoding, collision→overflow promotion). Ord impls: SchedulerEntry max-heap by priority, TimedEntry reversed for min-heap on deadline, both FIFO by generation. RNG tie-breaking collects same-priority/deadline entries, selects with modulo, pushes rest back. move_to_cancel_lane searches all lanes, retain+re-push. steal_ready_batch_into only steals from ready lane. pop_timed_only_with_hint safe because all collected entries share earliest deadline. ScheduleCertificate uses SipHash chaining. No concurrency - pure data structure with &mut self. ~40 tests cover all paths.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireHill","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:03:41.621475082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T02:06:08.145380116Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T02:06:08.145298754Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36ava","depends_on_id":"bd-2wds9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:03:41.630058868Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3rhs","title":"Platform reactors E2E test suite: cross-platform I/O verification","description":"# Platform Reactors E2E Test Suite

## Overview
Comprehensive test suite verifying reactor implementations work correctly on
all supported platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows).

## Test Directory Structure
```
tests/e2e/reactor/
├── mod.rs                    # Test module root
├── common/
│   ├── mod.rs                # Shared test utilities
│   └── tcp_echo.rs           # TCP echo server helper
├── trait_contract/
│   ├── register.rs           # Registration tests
│   ├── poll.rs               # Polling tests
│   ├── wake.rs               # Wakeup tests
│   └── lifecycle.rs          # Cleanup tests
├── tcp/
│   ├── connect.rs            # TCP connect tests
│   ├── accept.rs             # TCP accept tests
│   └── throughput.rs         # Throughput benchmarks
├── stress/
│   ├── many_connections.rs   # High connection count
│   ├── rapid_register.rs     # Fast register/deregister
│   └── concurrent_poll.rs    # Multi-threaded polling
└── platform_specific/
    ├── io_uring.rs           # Linux io_uring specific
    ├── epoll.rs              # Linux epoll specific
    ├── kqueue.rs             # macOS specific
    └── iocp.rs               # Windows specific
```

## Test Categories

### 1. Trait Contract Tests
Verify all reactors implement the Reactor trait correctly:
- register() adds source to reactor
- reregister() modifies interest
- deregister() removes source
- poll() returns ready events
- wake() interrupts blocked poll()

### 2. TCP Integration Tests
Real TCP operations through reactor:
- Client connect (non-blocking)
- Server accept (listener)
- Read/write cycles
- Concurrent connections

### 3. Stress Tests
- 10K+ connections simultaneously
- Rapid register/deregister cycling
- Multi-threaded poll() calls
- Memory pressure scenarios

### 4. Platform-Specific Tests
Tests that only run on specific platforms:
- io_uring: sqpoll, registered buffers
- epoll: EPOLLET edge-triggered behavior
- kqueue: kevent batching
- IOCP: overlapped I/O completion

## CI Matrix

### GitHub Actions Configuration
```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    os: [ubuntu-latest, ubuntu-20.04, macos-latest, windows-latest]
    rust: [stable, nightly]
    include:
      - os: ubuntu-latest
        features: io-uring
      - os: ubuntu-20.04
        features: epoll-fallback  # Older kernel without io_uring
```

## Logging Requirements

### Log Fields
```rust
tracing::info!(
    reactor_type = %reactor_name,
    platform = %std::env::consts::OS,
    connection_count = count,
    poll_latency_us = latency.as_micros(),
    events_per_poll = events,
    "Reactor test metrics"
);
```

### Artifact Collection
- Per-test timing data
- Event histograms
- Memory usage snapshots
- Platform-specific diagnostics

## Test Scripts

### Run All Platform Tests
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

export RUST_LOG="asupersync::reactor=debug,test=info"

echo "=== Platform Reactor Tests ==="
echo "Platform: $(uname -s)"
echo "Kernel: $(uname -r)"

# Detect available reactor
if [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Linux" ]]; then
    if [[ $(uname -r | cut -d. -f1) -ge 5 ]]; then
        echo "Reactor: io_uring (preferred)"
    else
        echo "Reactor: epoll (fallback)"
    fi
elif [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
    echo "Reactor: kqueue"
fi

cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_reactor -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee reactor_tests.log
```

 {"id":"bd-3rih","title":"[ALIEN][SCHED][LYAP] Efficient RuntimeSnapshot extraction (no hot-path allocs)","description":"## Purpose

The governor needs a snapshot of Σ (runtime state) to compute V(Σ).

We must compute this cheaply:
- no per-poll allocations
- stable deterministic counts

## Snapshot contents (candidate)

- outstanding obligations by kind
- pending cancel requests / acks
- deadline slack summary
- IO in-flight counts
- ready queue depth signals

## Acceptance criteria

- Snapshot can be computed with bounded work.
- Snapshot values are deterministic under lab runtime.","design":"### Implementation plan

Add a deterministic, allocation-free constructor for the Lyapunov governor snapshot.

**Location:** `src/obligation/lyapunov.rs` (`impl StateSnapshot`), because the type's docs already claim it can be constructed from `RuntimeState`.

### Snapshot extraction semantics (exact)

Given a `RuntimeState` with logical time `now = state.now`:

- `time`: `now`
- `live_tasks`: count of `state.tasks` whose `TaskState` is *not* terminal (`!task.state.is_terminal()`).
- `pending_obligations`: count of `state.obligations` where `obligation.is_pending()` (i.e. `ObligationState::Reserved`).
- `obligation_age_sum_ns`: `sum now.duration_since(obligation.reserved_at)` over pending obligations.
- `draining_regions`: count of `state.regions` with `RegionState` in `{Draining, Finalizing}`.
- `deadline_pressure`: for each **live** task with a deadline in its `CxInner` budget:
  - `slack_ns = deadline.duration_since(now)` (saturating at 0 when overdue)
  - `term = max(0, 1 - slack_ns / D0)`
  - `deadline_pressure = sum term`

`D0` is a fixed normalization constant (chosen as 1s) to make the signal represent "tasks due soon or overdue" rather than far-future deadlines.

### Determinism + performance constraints

- **No allocations on the hot path:** no `Vec`, no `HashMap`, no `collect`.
- **Deterministic iteration:** only scan arenas in their natural order; no hash iteration.
- **Graceful degradation:** if a task's `CxInner` lock is poisoned, skip its deadline contribution (do not panic).

### Follow-ups (if needed)

If scanning overhead becomes noticeable in profiling, add incremental counters (updated on task/obligation/region transitions) so snapshots become O(1).
","acceptance_criteria":"- `StateSnapshot::from_runtime_state(&RuntimeState)` exists and is `#[must_use]`.
- Snapshot extraction is deterministic and allocation-free.
- Unit tests cover:
  - live task counting
  - pending obligation counting + age sum
  - draining/finalizing region counting
  - deadline pressure for both "due soon" and "overdue" cases
- `cargo test -p asupersync snapshot_from_runtime` passes (once repo-wide compile blockers are cleared).
","notes":"### Progress

- Implemented `StateSnapshot::from_runtime_state` + unit tests in `src/obligation/lyapunov.rs`.

### Current blocker

- Repo currently fails to compile tests due to a stale `TraceEventKind::ObligationAcquire` reference in `src/trace/certificate.rs` (should be `ObligationReserve`). I've contacted the current holder of the `src/trace/**` reservation to patch/release.
